data.title: Viking Therapeutics net loss widens, cash position falls
data.description:
• Viking Therapeutics (VKTX) saw its year-over-year GAAP EPS loss in Q1 widen to ($1.37) from ($0.41).
  • The biopharma focused on metabolic and endocrine disorders was negatively impacted in the quarter by a near YoY tripling of total operating expenses to ~$164.1M from ~$55.5M. R&D costs accounted for ~$150.2M of the Q1 2026 figure.
  • At the end of the quarter (March 31), Viking had cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments of $603M compared to $706M on Dec. 31, 2025.
  • Viking provided updates on one of its key programs, VK2735, a dual GLP-1/GIP agonist for weight loss. Initiation of a phase 3 trial for the oral version is projected in Q4, and data from a maintenance dosing trial is expected in Q3.

data.title: Primaris Real Estate Investment Trust reports Q1 results

data.title: Meta in charts: R&D expenses rise 46% to $17.7B in Q1
First Mid announces CEO transition as part of planned succession
data.description:
• First Mid Bancshares (Nasdaq: FMBH) on Wednesday said it will appoint Matt Smith as Chief Executive Officer and President effective July 1, 2026, as part of a planned leadership transition.
  • The company said current CEO Joe Dively will move into the role of Executive Chairman and continue to oversee board strategy, governance, and mergers and acquisitions activity.
  • First Mid said Smith, currently President, has been with the company since 2016 and previously served as CFO.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Brera Holdings appoints board member Ron Sade as new CEO to drive growth
data.description:
• Brera Holdings (SLMT) announced on Wednesday that it has appointed Ron Sade as CEO, effective May 1, 2026.
  • He has been on the company’s board since Sept. 2025 and brings experience in tech investments and growth strategy.
  • The company said he will focus on improving operations and strengthening its market and compliance position, while Ron Sade added he looks forward to working with the team to support the company’s goals and regain full listing compliance.
  • The stock plunged ~3.21% to $0.73.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Equinix stock plunges as Q1 earnings fall short of expectations
data.description:
Equinix (EQIX) stock dipped after the data center REIT's first-quarter earnings missed analyst estimates even as the full-year guidance was raised.

Shares were 5.24% lower at $1,032.00 during Wednesday after-market trading.

The company reported a Q1 adjusted FFO per share of $10.79, up 12% year-over-year but below the Visible Alpha consensus estimate of $11.04.

Revenue increased 10% from the prior-year quarter to $2.44B, against the $2.52B consensus.

Adjusted EBITDA came in at $1.25B, an increase of 17% from the same period a year ago.

"We are raising our 2026 financial outlook based on the underlying strength of our Q1 performance and disciplined execution by our teams. The essential infrastructure we provide is enabling companies to accelerate innovation and enhancing our market position," said CEO Adaire Fox-Martin.

The full-year 2026 revenue guidance was raised to a range of $10.14B-10.24B from $10.12B-1.22B, vs. $10.22B consensus.

AFFO per share is now expected to be in the range of $42.31-$43.11, compared to the prior guidance of $41.93-$42.74 and against the $42.65 consensus.

Adjusted EBITDA guidance is raised to $5.17B-$5.25B from $5.14B-$5.22B.

For the second quarter of 2026, the company expects revenue to be between $2.57B and $2.61B, compared to the average analyst estimate of $2.52B.

Earnings call is scheduled for 5:30 PM ET today.

data.title: Choice Properties Real Estate Investment Trust reports Q1 results
